item.title: How do I write like this?
item.content:encoded: How exactly do I write like this? I already read ACS, but my paragraphs on the draft don't look like this. I'd like to know how to do this! Thanks!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I personally really like the use of the trick ending or redacted truth sorta thing in most scps that use it but it seems very underwhelming in this one. like the entire article builds them up as giant things that can destroy large amounts of the world and then in the end it just reiterates it like some sorta revalation

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like it, it’s short but effective. You conveyed enough sense of doom in the first part, but the second part was what really launched it. The idea that dozens of these things are sleeping away, ready to awaken, is terrifying. I also liked how you linked it to the burning of Alexandria, these things really have ravaged humanity for all of history

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like the sense of impending doom youve created!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1. Ok, some basic feedback, less than what I'd probably give in a draft forum.

+ show block
– hide block

BIG PICTURE PROBLEM, this doesn't feel like a fully developed idea. "Big underground multi-limb reptiles" is kind of an interesting mental image, but not enough to fully carry this. The biggest trait about this thing is that it's dangerous, potentially catastrophic, but danger isn't very interesting. Tying it to other real eruptions and implying it could erupt soon/faster are both part of that "woo danger" thing, so they don't add much. You spend a lot of time spelling out the past breaches, but you don't go beyond just mentioning the narrative elements of past eruptions: What happened when they erupted in the past (except that it kills things)? Why do they erupt? Why do they follow a schedule? There could be interesting answers behind those questions, but you don't address any of them, or you noncommittally mention theories. Why should I care about past or future eruptions? "It could destroy human society" is a little interesting the way you describe it, but right now that's just more danger and it's in a sea of bland.

The reveal that there are many in other volcanos across the world was actually really cool, but it didn't address the gap in the equation.

ON TO SPECIFICS:
Your containment procedures are way too long and full of uninteresting detail. Compared to similar SCPs, you've got more than twice as much as the (similarly dangerous) SCP-169. Less than SCP-1179, but that one uses the containment procedures as a storytelling device, yours doesn't. Figure out how many times you're saying the same thing different ways (we can't contain this, there's going to be a disaster, etc.), pick the best one or two ways, and move the hell on.

The Greek letters paragraph is kind of pointless. I'm ok with jargon, but why designate these things? And I don't mean why in-universe, I mean why are you taking my time as a reader to insert a letter, explain that letter, and then use it in a SINGLE sentence? If you have a good answer for that, great, write to fit that. If not, why is it there?

Also, there's no real build-up. Your containment starts with "it's gonna blow up society!", where can you go from there?

Probably my first major issue was with the description, where you're just kind of spelling out what they "believe" this thing looks like. It's like… you're giving too much detail for the situation of vagueness you've already set up. I feel like not only are you doing a lot of plain exposition (telling not showing) throughout the article, it's speculative plain exposition, which is somehow worse. Especially for the description, just write it as if they somehow know this stuff, or go with "we can't detect much" and don't go into the detail.

Split your sentences up. I don't think they're technically run-on, but you've got like 4-5 clauses in some of these.

Probably the second big problem, writing wise:

Between giant lizards, evil staircases, and god knows what else running around on the surface the thought of yet another thing to worry about seemed far from favorable.
You know the mantra, “we die in the dark, so the rest of humanity can live in the light,"

Referencing the most popular articles for flavor alone gives the impression that you've read about 5 SCPs total and wanted to reference them all. Whether or not that's true, it drastically undercuts people's ability to take this seriously.
1032 and 2000 were more relevant, but 1032 feels a bit random when there aren't any other crosslinks and the 2000 sentence definitely didn't pack enough of a punch to deserve its own level 5 collapsible. (And why be cagey about the link in a level 5 collapsible?) It would be much better if integrated into the procedures or the previous collapsible.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Noted and amended accordingly. Thank you for the input!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The use of nuclear and anomalous weaponry has been approved for such uses

"Use for such uses"
"Such uses" should be "this purpose" maybe?

But you know the mantra, “we die in the dark, so the rest of humanity can live in the light;” but sooner or later

Double but. Second one is unnecessary. You could remove the first one instead, but then I think the semicolon is unnecessary, and should be a comma.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: for the record, I didn't actually give a full critique on this, I just addressed a problematic post in the forum thread in my capacity as Forum Crit staff, but thank you for the mention anyway I appreciate it!!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The illustrations are really impressive, and the whole thing is well written, but I was expecting there to be more to the idea or for some kind of plot to unfold. This also does a lot of telling instead of showing, especially through the addenda. It balances out to a novote for me, but I like where this is coming from and respect the effort behind it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry for taking the spot you had you eye on, but thanks for the feedback! Good luck with your SCP!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I tell ya, there must be something in the water…a draft I'm working on features the words "lithosphere" "asthenosphere" and "tectonic", cross-references black-boxed SCPs, and was targeting the 4997 slot. (Begrudgingly, I believe I'll have to find another SCP slot.)

+ show block
– hide block

A couple of split hairs:

The black-boxing in the article I just don't get. There's a lot of it and I'm not sure why. The number of limbs the creature has is the prime example; why does that need to be redacted? Am I missing something? If it is all just for mystery, I don't think it has an effect as strong as a real numbers would.

Additional theories claim it may be photosynthetic

A Foundation scientist thinks a subterranean creature that hasn't surfaced for 2 million years might be photosynthetic?

SCP-4997 is coming off of a dormant period

I think the clinical tone wavers here.

I think the Note from Dr. P█████ makes him seem kinda like a doofus. "magnitude, pun intended…", it's exactly what a goofball would write down. I mean that is okay if that's what you're going for.

I'm not so sure about the Level 5 collapsible. Seems anticlimactic and that it isn't really all that hush; could be included in the description where the initiative to neutralize the anomaly is mentioned. Seems odd that the O5 would be using the article as essentially a mailbox too.

There's room for some improvement, but the original pictures, “asthenobiology,” the technical competence of the work, and the first sentence of the third paragraph got you the +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The research paid off because I think it really sells this SCP. Slightly reminiscent of 169 but different enough to stand apart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I want to thank Luk9gamer10, Zyn who I am now realizing didn't actually critique it, and keyii for their feedback during the creation of this article; they were truly indispensable.

I’d always thought about the aura of majestic dread surrounding the Yellowstone Volcano and decided to put a name to that dread — SCP-4997! A lot of research went into this SCP and all the images were homemade, so there’s no copyright to worry about. If you feel the need to downvote, let me know what drove you to it.

I hope you all enjoy it!

Bonus: Thank you so much to u/jeandepain on Reddit for their amazing fan art which can be found here! Don't forget ToonHolt's fan art of 4997 here too!

Although the actual appearance is never described in-depth I wrote this with the image of a Colubrim in mind but you're free to interpret the text as you wish, especially since I like ToonHolt's iteration a lot more than my base concept, ily guys.
